title: Theklaï؟½s Answer
content:
آ آ آ آ Where I am, thou askï؟½st, and where I wended
آ آ آ آ When my fleeting shadow passï؟½d from thee?
آ آ آ آ Am I not concluded now, and ended?
آ آ آ آ Have not life and love been granted me?

آ آ آ آ Ask, where now those nightingales are singing,
آ آ آ آ Who, of late, on the soft nights of May,
آ آ آ آ Set thine ears with soul-fraught music ringing
آ آ آ آ Only, while their love livï؟½d, lasted they.

آ آ آ آ Find I him, from whom I had to sever?
آ آ آ آ Doubt it not, we met, and we are one.
آ آ آ آ There, where what is joinï؟½d, is joinï؟½d for ever,
آ آ آ آ There, where tears are never more to run.

آ آ آ آ There thou too shalt live with us together,
آ آ آ آ When thou too hast borne the love we bore:
آ آ آ آ There, from sin deliverï؟½d, dwells my Father,
آ آ آ آ Trackï؟½d by Murderï؟½s bloody sword no more.

آ آ آ آ There he feels, it was no dream deceiving
آ آ آ آ Lurï؟½d him starwards to uplift his eye:
آ آ آ آ God doth match his gifts to manï؟½s believing;
آ آ آ آ Believe, and thou shalt find the Holy nigh.

آ آ آ آ All thou augurest here of lovely seeming
آ آ آ آ There shall find fulfilment in its day:
آ آ آ آ Dare, O Friend, be wandering, dare be dreaming;
آ آ آ آ Lofty thought lies oft in childish play.
meaning:
This evocative piece by Matthew Arnold, titled "Theklaï؟½s Answer", represents a masterful exploration of classic. The lines capture a profound emotional resonance...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
